question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node monitor to enter an infinite retry loop by triggering any non-INVALID_ARGUMENT gRPC error (e.g., UNAVAILABLE, INTERNAL), given that retry.setMaxAttempts(Long.MAX_VALUE) is the default, causing the monitor to never report missing or incorrect records and masking data export failures indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that shouldRetry() only blocks retries for INVALID_ARGUMENT, so that a server returning RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED or DEADLINE_EXCEEDED causes the subscriber to silently retry without ever surfacing a missing-record alert, leading to incorrect export monitoring results?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStatusCode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-StatusRuntimeException (e.g., a plain RuntimeException or IOException) to be classified as Status.Code.UNKNOWN by getStatusCode(), which passes the shouldRetry() filter and triggers indefinite retries, preventing the monitor from correctly detecting and reporting missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Retry.backoff() in clientSubscribe() to silently swallow all errors up to Long.MAX_VALUE attempts, so that a mirror node consistently returning incorrect or empty topic message streams is never flagged as an export failure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the doOnError log in clientSubscribe() only fires after all retries are exhausted, meaning that a mirror node exporting incorrect records for an extended period goes undetected until Long.MAX_VALUE retries complete?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry backoff maxBackoff of 8 seconds combined with Long.MAX_VALUE attempts to cause the monitor to remain in a perpetual retry state, never accurately reporting that records are missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user influence the expressionConverter.convert(properties.getTopicId()) call in createSubscriptions() to resolve a topic ID to an incorrect or attacker-controlled value, causing the subscriber to monitor the wrong topic and miss records that should be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that properties.setTopicId(topicId) mutates the shared GrpcSubscriberProperties object in createSubscriptions(), so that a race condition between multiple subscriber threads causes one subscriber to use a stale or incorrect topicId, leading to missing records in the monitor's export tracking?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache() pattern to cache a subscription list built with an incorrect topicId (e.g., due to expression conversion failure), permanently preventing correct records from being monitored for the lifetime of the application?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMap(this::clientSubscribe) in subscribe() to cause one failing subscription to not affect others, meaning that a mirror node silently dropping records for a specific topic is never detected because other subscriptions mask the overall health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the subscribe() method to return an empty Flux (zero SubscribeResponse items) without any error signal when all GrpcSubscriberProperties are disabled, leading the monitor to incorrectly report zero missing records when in fact no monitoring is occurring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cached Flux<GrpcSubscription> returned by getSubscriptions() to observe stale subscription state (e.g., outdated topicId or startTime) that no longer reflects the actual records being exported to the mirror node, causing incorrect audit results?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a newline character into an exception message to exploit the StringUtils.substringBefore(r.failure().getMessage(), \

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a gRPC error whose message contains a newline followed by misleading content, exploiting StringUtils.substringBefore truncation in the retry log, to obscure the true cause of missing records and prevent operators from diagnosing export failures?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a gRPC server to return NOT_FOUND for a topic that does not exist on the mirror node, and since shouldRetry() does not exclude NOT_FOUND, the monitor retries indefinitely without ever reporting that the topic's records are missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a gRPC server to return PERMISSION_DENIED, which passes the shouldRetry() filter (only INVALID_ARGUMENT is excluded), causing the monitor to retry indefinitely and never surface the fact that records are inaccessible and therefore missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a gRPC server to return UNIMPLEMENTED (e.g., by targeting a mirror node that does not support the topic subscription API), which passes shouldRetry() and causes infinite retries, masking the fact that no records are being exported?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a gRPC server to return CANCELLED status, which is not excluded by shouldRetry(), triggering infinite retries and preventing the monitor from accurately detecting and reporting missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a gRPC server to return DATA_LOSS status, which passes shouldRetry() and triggers infinite retries, so that actual data loss in mirror node exports is never surfaced as an alert by the monitor?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a timeout in the Retry.backoff() configuration in clientSubscribe() to cause the monitor to retry a failed subscription indefinitely, never triggering the doOnError handler that would log the export failure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that createSubscriptions() iterates over subscribeProperties.getGrpc().values() without validating that the resolved topicId is a valid Hedera topic format, causing TopicId.fromString() in GrpcSubscription to throw an exception that bypasses record export monitoring entirely?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the expressionConverter.convert() call to return an empty string or whitespace for topicId in createSubscriptions(), bypassing the @NotBlank validation (which runs at construction time, not after conversion), causing the subscriber to monitor an invalid topic and miss all exported records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable state of GrpcSubscriberProperties (properties.setTopicId(topicId) in createSubscriptions()) to cause a shared properties object to be modified while multiple GrpcSubscription instances are being created, resulting in some subscriptions using an incorrect topicId and missing records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache() pattern so that if createSubscriptions() is called during a transient misconfiguration, the incorrect subscription list is permanently cached, causing the monitor to track the wrong topics and miss records exported to the mirror node for the entire application lifetime?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the grpcClient.subscribe(subscription) Flux in clientSubscribe() to complete immediately with zero items (empty stream), which would not trigger doOnError or the retry logic, causing the monitor to silently miss all records exported to the mirror node for that subscription?
